candidate.demo_mechanism: One-command installer as the demo: `curl -fsSL https://cctx.dev/install.sh | bash`. No hosted sandbox. Users install locally in 30 seconds and get a SQLite file at ~/.cctx/ they fully own. Demo is a 90-second terminal recording showing the install + immediate cross-tool context transfer.
candidate.business_model: Open-core: MIT kernel with full self-host capability + optional $9/mo managed Turso sync tier for teams. The self-host path is genuinely viable (SQLite file you can grep, backup with cp, sync yourself). Paid tier is pure convenience, not a feature gate. Matches the minimalism thesis because the core is just a file you own, and monetization doesn't compromise that.
candidate.pricing: $0 self-host (MIT, no enforcement) + $9/mo managed Turso sync for teams wanting multi-device/multi-person without running their own infra. No free-tier limits on self-host. Paid is opt-in convenience.
candidate.most_effective_demo: A 90-second screen recording: (1) Claude Code conversation where you create a TODO list, (2) close Claude Code, (3) open Cursor the next morning, (4) Cursor instantly recalls the exact TODO without manual prompting, (5) show `ls -lh ~/.cctx/memory.db` proving it's just a 2MB SQLite file you own. Time-to-aha: 60 seconds (the moment Cursor recalls context from Claude Code). Audience: developers using 2+ AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) frustrated that context doesn't transfer and tired of re-explaining project state.
candidate.followership_channel: Show HN Tuesday 9am EST with title 'cctx – cross-AI memory in a SQLite file you own' + simultaneous cross-post to r/ClaudeAI and r/LocalLLaMA with the same 90-second recording. HN rewards 'you can own this' pitches and self-hostable tools. The Claude subreddit is where users actively complain about context loss between sessions. LocalLLaMA audience values data ownership. All three channels convert on the same hook: Anthropic just shipped memory_stores (cloud-locked, no export), and here's the self-host alternative that works across any AI tool, not just Claude.
candidate.precedent: Plausible Analytics. Their 'I self-host this' blog posts from early users worked because the self-host path was genuinely usable and people could verify ownership by pointing at their own instance. cctx's installer delivers the same experience: you get a SQLite file at ~/.cctx/ that you can inspect, backup, or sync however you want. Anthropic's memory_stores cannot run this play because they're API-only with no local replica or export.
candidate.reasoning: The installer-as-demo positions cctx as 'memory you own' against Anthropic's newly-shipped memory_stores (cloud-locked, no export path). Show HN and the Claude/LocalLLaMA subreddits are exactly where developers discuss context portability and data ownership. The open-core model with genuine self-host matches Plausible's playbook: free users become advocates ('I grep my memory file'), teams that want managed sync convert to $9/mo for convenience. Every field reinforces 'fewer moving parts, you own the file.' Swap the demo to a hosted sandbox and the ownership claim breaks; swap the channel to LinkedIn and the self-host audience disappears; swap pricing to $49/mo and HN smells like a commercial land-grab. The plan is internally load-bearing.

verdict.feasibility_reason: One operator can ship in 2 days: existing installer + 90-sec recording + HN/Reddit posts; $0 new spend, respects $5/mo API cap.
verdict.differentiation_reason: Weaponizes minimalism thesis ('SQLite file you own') against memory_stores; Plausible precedent specifically transfers (self-host → advocacy posts); competitors can't copy.
verdict.coherence_reason: Every field is load-bearing: swap demo to hosted breaks ownership claim, swap channel to LinkedIn loses self-host audience, swap pricing breaks HN perception.
verdict.constraint_violation_reason: No violations: no ads, no new features, no hosted demo burning API tokens, executable by solo operator, fits free-tier infra.
